prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `f3e62deb`.
codex_response:
Implemented `f3e62deb` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f3e62deb/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f3e62deb/verifier.py), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_f3e62deb/generator.py). The rule is: move the single hollow `3x3` square to the edge determined by its color, preserving shape and the perpendicular coordinate: `6 -> top`, `8 -> right`, `4 -> bottom`, and other foreground colors -> left. The Opus summary matched the official examples, so I used it; `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` had no entry. No new primitive was needed.

`conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.verify --task f3e62deb` passed `8/8` official examples, and `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.build --task f3e62deb --n-examples 1000` completed successfully. The generator stays in the same family by using one interior hollow square and color-coded edge translation, while varying square canvas size to make `1000` unique verified examples possible. I also opened [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/f3e62deb/previews/preview_01.png) and it looks consistent with the task.
